source_pr.title: *: Fix data race in TestSetLabelsConcurrentWithGetLabel
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/45578

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Go race detector reported a data race in concurrent label updates vs global config reads, and the regression test itself required stable goroutine termination (server/http_handler.go, server/http_handler_test.go).
failure_signature: DATA RACE: concurrent reads of global config and in-place mutation of `cfg.Labels` map during label updates; plus potential instability in the test's background goroutine stop signal.
root_cause_explanation: The race comes from updating the global config labels map in-place while other goroutines read config concurrently (maps are not safe for concurrent mutation). The fix avoids mutating the existing `cfg.Labels` map: it merges old labels into a new map and then replaces `cfg.Labels` atomically with the new map before storing the global config. The regression test exercises concurrent POST /labels and config reads, and it also tightens the stop signaling for the background goroutine to keep the test itself stable (server/http_handler.go, server/http_handler_test.go).
fix_pattern: Avoid in-place mutation of shared global maps; replace `cfg.Labels` atomically, and make concurrency tests use a robust goroutine stop signal to avoid test-level races.

evidence: evidence: *: Fix data race in TestSetLabelsConcurrentWithGetLabel
